scope.summary: Document and stub the LiveSync / EveryWear-equivalent real-time engine link as explicitly deferred post-v1, implement a watched-folder live-sync bridge for UE that auto-pushes the latest promoted garment USD/glTF to a watched UE Content directory on promotion, and record the MOAT-7 deferred-requirement note in T-PIPELINE-INTEROP
acceptance_criteria: The UE export bridge (MT-254) is extended so that when auto_sync=true is set in the export request, any TailorGarmentPromoted event triggers an automatic re-export to the configured UE watched folder without operator action
The auto-sync path uses the existing export job infrastructure (MT-318 scheduler) and emits a TailorGarmentExportCompleted event with sync_target='ue_watched_folder'
Full LiveSync (live in-editor mesh preview while authoring in Tailor) is noted as DEFERRED in the ModelManual and T-PIPELINE-INTEROP; the workaround is auto_sync promotion export

Full EveryWear equivalence (auto-rig, LOD generation, texture bake) is noted as MOAT-7 DEFERRED post-v1 in both the ModelManual entry and the T-PIPELINE-INTEROP spec sub-section with a reference to this MT
Unity engine path is noted as a consumer of the standard glTF/GLB export (MT-249) and USD export (MT-250); no Unity-specific bridge is implemented in v1 and this is documented
scope.risk_if_missed: Without any promotion-triggered UE sync, every iteration cycle requires a manual export step between Tailor and Unreal Engine, making the tool impractical for game-engine character-art pipelines where artists iterate rapidly. The gap also means MOAT-7 is never formally noted as a deferred requirement in T-PIPELINE-INTEROP, risking it being forgotten.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
